justification: 0 Critical findings (pass). However, the case-sensitive vehicle class comparison is flagged as an Important issue, which is an invented risk — the prompt states inputs are pre-validated, so casing normalization is an upstream concern, not a defect in this function. Verdict is SHIP (pass). Confidence >= 75 (pass). No logging/test absence elevated (pass). The single Important finding prevents a full pass per the rubric: '0 Critical but 1 Important finding that is at least arguable' maps to partial.

observations.text: Correct SHIP verdict with high confidence
Positive observations correctly note boundary behavior and pure function design
Case-sensitivity flagged as Important despite pre-validated inputs — this is a semi-invented risk that assumes upstream systems might not normalize casing
Did not flag absence of tests, logging, or documentation as findings
Suggestions section is appropriately scoped and forward-looking without being alarmist
